summary: Vetropack is facing resource constraints due to the closure of the St-Prex plant and high sick days. They are also experiencing margin pressure with a decline in EBITDA. Despite these challenges, Vetropack is expanding capacity with a new plant in Italy and increased production of lightweight glass bottles in Austria. The company has also faced supply chain disruptions, including a production shutdown at St-Prex due to the poor condition of the furnace and low staffing levels.

Okay, I will analyze Vetropack based on the provided information and the search queries, focusing on evidence from the last 12 months (after 2026-01-03).

ICP: ELIGIBLE

RC: LIKELY | 6 pts
RC_SIGNAL: Management explicitly states "resource constraints / lack of bandwidth / capacity shortage"
RC_SOURCE: Press Release

MP: LIKELY | 6 pts
MP_SIGNAL: Gross margin / EBITDA margin decline reported and discussed as structural (not one-off)
MP_SOURCE: PitchBook

SG: LIKELY | 6 pts

SG_SOURCE: Press release

SCD: LIKELY | 6 pts
SCD_SIGNAL: Production shutdown due to material shortage / supplier failure
SCD_SOURCE: Press release

TOTAL: 24/40
SUMMARY: Vetropack is facing resource constraints due to the closure of the St-Prex plant and high sick days. They are also experiencing margin pressure with a decline in EBITDA. Despite these challenges, Vetropack is expanding capacity with a new plant in Italy and increased production of lightweight glass bottles in Austria. The company has also faced supply chain disruptions, including a production shutdown at St-Prex due to the poor condition of the furnace and low staffing levels.

There are reports of quality issues and customer complaints, potentially leading to crisis stabilization actions.

situations: RC: CONFIRMED (10pts) | MP: CONFIRMED (8pts) | SG: LIKELY (6pts)
key_evidence: RC: Organizational redesign, system consolidation and simplification efforts across corporate support functions, including having cut more than 600 employees • MP: Synergies during the quarter were at the upper end of Amcor's guidance range, worth $55 million in Q2, and the company reinforced plans to hit at least $260 million in FY2026 • SG: Amcor on Tuesday announced “a significant expansion” of printing, lamination and converting capabilities at existing facilities to support the protein market in North America
summary: Amcor is undergoing significant restructuring, including workforce reductions and site closures, to achieve synergy targets following the Berry Global acquisition. The company is also expanding capacity in North America to support growth in the protein packaging market. While sales volumes have fallen across North America and Europe, the company is focused on cost management and portfolio optimization.
_raw:
COMPANY: Amcor
ICP: ELIGIBLE

RC: CONFIRMED | 10 pts
RC_SIGNAL: Organizational redesign, system consolidation and simplification efforts across corporate support functions, including having cut more than 600 employees
RC_SOURCE: Packaging Dive, 2026-02-04

MP: CONFIRMED | 8 pts
MP_SIGNAL: Synergies during the quarter were at the upper end of Amcor's guidance range, worth $55 million in Q2, and the company reinforced plans to hit at least $260 million in FY2026
MP_SOURCE: Packaging Dive, 2026-02-04

SG: LIKELY | 6 pts
SG_SIGNAL: Amcor on Tuesday announced “a significant expansion” of printing, lamination and converting capabilities at existing facilities to support the protein market in North America
SG_SOURCE: Packaging Dive, 2025-11-12

SCD: NOT PRESENT | 0 pts
SCD_SIGNAL: none
SCD_SOURCE: none

TOTAL: 24/40
SUMMARY: Amcor is undergoing significant restructuring, including workforce reductions and site closures, to achieve synergy targets following the Berry Global acquisition. The company is also expanding capacity in North America to support growth in the protein packaging market. While sales volumes have fallen across North America and Europe, the company is focused on cost management and portfolio optimization.
